senses.definition: a sport where two teams aim to land discs on each other's court sections

examples.x: Teams in double disc court compete to score points by hitting their opponents' court.
Double disc court requires precise throws and quick reflexes.
The rules of double disc court emphasize teamwork and strategic positioning.
Double disc court tournaments attract skilled athletes from around the world.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of attempting to catch a disc thrown at them without letting it hit the ground

examples.x: Their team won the guts match by catching the disc after a long rally.
Playing guts requires quick reflexes and strong teamwork.
Guts tournaments often draw enthusiastic crowds of disc sports fans.
The rules of guts stipulate that players must catch the disc cleanly without dropping it.
In guts, accuracy in throwing the disc is just as important as catching it.

senses.definition: a throw in flying disc sports where the disc is released with its outer edge tilted downward, causing it to curve left or right

examples.x: He executed a perfect hyzer shot around the tree, landing the disc near the basket.
He prefers a hyzer shot on this hole due to the natural curve of the fairway.
The wind forced her hyzer shot to veer sharply left, missing the intended target.
The hyzer shot requires precise control of the disc's angle to achieve the desired curve.
She used a hyzer shot to avoid the water hazard and position the disc close to the pin.

senses.definition: (flying disc sport) a throw where the disc is released with its outer edge tilted upward relative to the ground

examples.x: The disc took a sharp left turn after he released it with a slight anhyzer.
Using an anhyzer with a stable disc helped him manage the strong headwind.
He threw an anhyzer to curve around the tree and land near the basket.
The windy conditions forced her to use an anhyzer to avoid turning the disc over too much.
Learning to control an anhyzer can greatly improve your game on tricky courses.

senses.definition: (flying disc sport) an attempt by a player to catch or intercept the disc

examples.x: His bid was unsuccessful as the disc sailed just out of reach.
She made a spectacular bid for the disc, diving full stretch.
We need a strong bid from our defense to regain possession.
Her bid in the end zone secured a crucial point for the team.
The bid for the long throw fell short due to the wind.

senses.definition: a non-contact team sport played with a flying disc where players score points by catching the disc in the opposing team's end zone

examples.x: The championship game of ultimate frisbee is next weekend.
He dove to catch the frisbee during an ultimate frisbee match.
We play ultimate frisbee every Sunday at the park.
Playing ultimate frisbee is a fun way to stay active.
We need more players for our ultimate frisbee team.

senses.definition: a sport or activity where players throw a flying disc into a series of baskets, aiming to complete each hole in the fewest throws

examples.x: Disc golf combines the elements of frisbee throwing and traditional golf.
The professional disc golf tournament attracted top players from around the country.
Her accuracy in disc golf improved after hours of practice.
The disc golf community organized a charity event to raise funds for local youth programs.
Disc golf etiquette includes being mindful of other players on the course.

senses.definition: a discipline in flying disc where athletes perform tricks and routines with a flying disc, emphasizing creativity and skill

examples.x: He practiced flying disc freestyle every afternoon to perfect his throws.
The world championship of flying disc freestyle drew competitors from across the globe.
She amazed the audience with her agility and precision in flying disc freestyle.
Learning flying disc freestyle requires mastering various spins and catches.
Advanced flying disc freestyle routines often incorporate music and choreography.

senses.definition: the initial throw to start a game or point in ultimate frisbee

examples.x: She aimed for maximum distance with her pull.
They practiced their pulls for hours to perfect their technique.
He executed a powerful pull that soared across the field.
The audience cheered as the pull sailed gracefully through the air.
His accurate pull landed right in the hands of a teammate.

senses.definition: a defensive maneuver in frisbee where a player guards the thrower closely to prevent easy throws

examples.x: He applied a tight mark, making it difficult for the thrower to find an open pass.
The mark forced a stall count by preventing any immediate throws.
The mark forced a turnover with a well-timed layout block.
The handler struggled to break the strong mark applied by the defender.
A good mark can disrupt the offense's flow and force errors.

senses.definition: (flying disc sport) the act of a player diving horizontally to catch or intercept the disc

examples.x: She executed a flawless layout to secure the point.
They practiced layouts to improve their defensive skills.
A successful layout requires speed and agility.
The highlight reel showed incredible layouts from both teams.

senses.definition: (disc golf) the act of completing a hole with a single throw, landing the disc directly in the target

examples.x: Achieving an ace requires precise aim and technique.
He celebrated his ace with a triumphant shout.
Her first ace was a memorable moment in her disc golf career.
They cheered loudly when he landed an ace.
His ace on the final hole secured his victory.

senses.definition: a type of throw in flying disc sports where the disc is released upside down with an overhand motion

examples.x: He mastered the scoober to get around tight defenses.
Her scoober sailed perfectly into the end zone.
A well-placed scoober can confuse even the best defenders.
His scoober passed over the defender's head with ease.
Their coach taught them the scoober as a secret weapon.
